description:
GOAL: Make the IR-shaped typer flow-sensitive enough to narrow types through structural pattern operations. After this ticket, downstream consumers can rely on tighter Descrs at branch successors (e.g. tuple fields, list head/tail, map values, bitstring fields, post-If branches).

## Why this is atomic

After this ticket, type_module produces tighter Descrs at branches and pattern projections. Existing .11.24.2 unit tests still pass; new tests exercise the narrowing. No consumer wiring yet — that's .11.24.4 / .11.24.5 / .11.24.6.

## Deliverables

src/ir_typer.rs:
- Switch driver from flow-insensitive (single pass over blocks) to flow-sensitive: per-block entry env, propagated through stmts, joined at block params.
- Narrowing rules:
  * `Term::If(cond, then_b, else_b)`: if `cond` was bound by a predicate prim (e.g. `ListIsNil(v)`), narrow v in then_b to nil; in else_b to non-nil ListSig. Same for `BinOp::Eq(v, Const(Int n))` -> v narrowed to int_lit(n) on truthy branch. Generalize via a small predicate-recognizer.
  * `Stmt::Let(h, ListHead(v))`: h gets list_element_type(env[v]); env[v] retains list shape (no change).
  * `Stmt::Let(t, ListTail(v))`: t gets non-empty-list-tail Descr (could be empty list, so list ∪ nil).
  * `Stmt::Let(f, TupleField(v, i))`: f gets tuple_projections(env[v], arity)[i].
  * `Stmt::Let(r, MapGet(m, k))`: r gets map_field_lookup(env[m], expr_to_map_key(k)) ∪ Nil.
  * `Stmt::Let(_, BitReadField{...})`: result tuple's success-branch field has the field's Descr (Int width / Float / Bitstring).
- Reuse existing pure helpers: pattern_type, pattern_bindings, tuple_projections, list_element_type, map_field_lookup, refine_map_field.
- Fixpoint on block params; converge in O(blocks * vars) for typical cases.

## Tests

- `fn f(t), do: TupleField(t, 0)` — when called with TupleSig{[Int, Atom]}, field 0 has Descr Int.
- `fn f(l) when ListIsNil(l), do: 0` — within the truthy branch body, l is narrowed to nil; calling list_element_type returns bottom there.
- `fn f(l), do: ListHead(l)` — head's Descr equals list_element_type(param).
- A case-style fn lowered to chained If's narrows correctly through each branch.
- A nested tuple destructure: `{ {a, b}, c }` produces correct per-field Descrs at each level.

## Out of scope

- Consumer wiring (.11.24.4-.7).
- Narrowing through closure invocation (.11.24.7's specialize_return makes this meaningful).
- Negative type information from guards more elaborate than direct predicates (e.g. arithmetic guard conditions narrowing operand types) — file follow-up if profiling shows it matters.

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.24.2.
- BLOCKS: fz-ul4.11.24.4, fz-ul4.11.24.5, fz-ul4.11.24.6, fz-ul4.11.24.7.
title: Pattern narrowing through IR pattern ops
